channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why enterprises fail at finops
item.description: As cloud spending continues to climb, the discipline known as finops (financial operations) has emerged as a critical strategy for managing and optimizing cloud investments. This is no longer news. Finops has been a discipline and a toolkit for years.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Everything missing from the iPhone 16e, including MagSafe and Photographic Styles
item.description: Yes, the iPhone 16e has similar looks to the iPhone 16, but here's everything that is missing between the two.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Grok 3 model puts xAI at the top tier of frontier model developers
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Perplexity releases a censorship-free variant of Deepseek R1
item.description: Perplexity is releasing its model R1 1776, a version of Deepseek R1 with open model weights that has been post-trained to remove China’s censorship and provide more unbiased, accurate answers, according to Perplexity co-founder and CEO Aravind Srinivas. He wrote about the move on LinkedIn.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Climate change is shrinking glaciers faster than ever, with 7 trillion tons lost since 2000
item.description: A new and massive study finds that climate change is melting the world’s mountain glaciers faster than ever.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Quantum computing breakthrough? Microsoft says its new Majorana 1 chip shows we’re closer than ever
item.description: Microsoft on Wednesday unveiled a new chip that it said showed quantum computing is “years, not decades” away, joining Google and IBM in predicting that a fundamental change in computing technology is much closer than recently believed.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Trump keeps cutting election security jobs. Here’s what’s at risk
item.description: As the Trump administration continues to dismantle federal agencies, one that plays a critical role in U.S. infrastructure and election security faces an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Revered turntable maker Pro-Ject just unveiled a complete vinyl package with amp and speakers, and I love its boldness
item.description: Pro-Ject has unveiled a pair new, ‘ready-made’ audio systems for vinyl fans that go big on colour and small on complexity.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Huge data leak exposes 14 million customer shipping records
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Hipshipper, which is used by eBay, Shopify and Amazon sellers, exposed millions of shipping labels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las aplicaciones empresariales obsoletas pueden nublar su visión de la IA
item.description: Las aplicaciones de software obsoletas están creando obstáculos para la adopción de la IA en muchas organizaciones, y la capacidad limitada de retención de datos es una de las principales causas, según los expertos en TI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google caught in crossfire over Gulf of America name change
item.description: Google has found itself back in the crosshairs of a geopolitical battle over President Trump’s renaming of the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America, raising fresh questions over the role technology companies can play in reinforcing political narratives.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab Launches, Escalating AI Talent War
item.description: Mira Murati's new startup Thinking Machines Lab joins the fight for top talent in the fast-evolving AI sector, with a focus on making AI systems more accessible and customizable.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s first step to scalable quantum computing
item.description: There’s a race going on. Not the one to build artificial general intelligence, whatever that may be, but to deliver the first at-scale quantum computer that’s able to solve a class of massive problems that would take far too long using even the most powerful supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The iPhone 16E is here. Here are 5 ways it’s different from the iPhone SE
item.description: Apple has introduced its first new product of 2025: the iPhone 16E. The new iPhone replaces the iPhone SE from Apple’s lineup—the company’s entry-level, budget iPhone.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft updates VS Code C# Dev Kit extension
item.description: Microsoft announced updates to its C# Dev Kit for Visual Studio Code extension, including enhancements to Razor/Blazor Hot Reload and for debugging and testing Blazor web pages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This AI trend lets TikTok users relive history’s best—and worst—moments
item.description: Ever wondered what it would be like to wake up in Pompei on eruption day? How about how it would have felt to be a passenger on the Titanic? Now you don’t need to.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk Reveals Why xAI Chose Memphis for World's Largest AI Supercomputer
item.description: Elon Musk announces why he chose Memphis, Tennessee, to host the world's largest artificial intelligence supercomputer and reveals his company's plans for a water recycling facility in the city.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Crackdown on power-guzzling data centers may soon come online in California
item.description: A spate of new bills in California and other states would protect consumers from electricity rate hikes that subsidize data centers.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: This AI tool could help curb domestic violence
item.description: A new technology can pinpoint victims of intimate partner violence four years earlier than other detection systems and with 80% accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Sibani Ram, Fast Company

item.title: VMware by Broadcom: Product, service and support news
item.description: News of Broadcom’s plans to acquire VMware triggered concerns among customers, partners, and industry watchers, many of whom recalled Broadcom’s track record with prior high-profile acquisitions, including network switching vendor Brocade in 2016 for almost $6 billion, software firm CA Technologies
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fortinet speeds threat detection with improved FortiAnalyzer
item.description: Fortinet has reinforced its FortiAnalyzer security analytics and log management platform with capabilities that enhance enterprise threat detection and automate incident response. FortiAnalyzer securely collects logs from Fortinet devices such as FortiGate firewalls and endpoint security agents.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Oracle extends support for on-prem Oracle Database 19c
item.description: As the status of an on-premises version of Oracle Database 23ai remains in limbo, the company has tossed a bone to its on-prem Oracle Database 19c customers in the form of an extension of Premier Support to Dec. 31, 2029, and Extended Support to Dec. 31, 2032.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4
